blurb: App code signing requires developers to obtain a signing certificate from Apple and use it to cryptographically sign all executable binaries and resources before distribution. On iOS and iPadOS, the AMFI kernel extension verifies the signature and entitlements of every app at launch, rejecting unsigned or improperly signed code. Provisioning profiles, signed by Apple, scope the capabilities an app may use and restrict which devices can run development builds. Apps distributed via the App Store are additionally signed by Apple with a distribution signature, providing an Apple-attested chain of trust from developer certificate through to device installation.
